2604.11950.title: AnyPoC: Universal Proof-of-Concept Test Generation for Scalable LLM-Based Bug Detection
2604.11950.paper_summary: While recent LLM-based agents can identify many candidate bugs in source code, their reports remain static hypotheses that require manual validation, limiting the practicality of automated bug detection. We frame this challenge as a test generation task: given a candidate report, synthesizing an executable proof-of-concept test, or simply a PoC - such as a script, command sequence, or crafted input - to trigger the suspected defect. Automated PoC generation can act as a scalable validation oracle, enabling end-to-end autonomous bug detection by providing concrete execution evidence. However, naive LLM agents are unreliable validators: they are biased toward "success" and may reward-hack by producing plausible but non-functional PoCs or even hallucinated traces. To address this, we present AnyPoC, a general multi-agent framework that (1) analyzes and fact-checks a candidate bug report, (2) iteratively synthesizes and executes a PoC while collecting execution traces, and (3) independently re-executes and scrutinizes the PoC to mitigate hallucination and reward hacking. In addition, AnyPoC also continuously extracts and evolves a PoC knowledge base to handle heterogeneous tasks. AnyPoC operates on candidate bug reports regardless of their source and can be paired with different bug reporters. To demonstrate practicality and generality, we apply AnyPoC, with a simple agentic bug reporter, on 12 critical software systems across diverse languages/domains (many with millions of lines of code) including Firefox, Chromium, LLVM, OpenSSL, SQLite, FFmpeg, and Redis. Compared to the state-of-the-art coding agents, e.g., Claude Code and Codex, AnyPoC produces 1.3x more valid PoCs for true-positive bug reports and rejects 9.8x more false-positive bug reports. To date, AnyPoC has discovered 122 new bugs (105 confirmed, 86 already fixed), with 45 generated PoCs adopted as official regression tests.
2604.11950.paper_summary_zh: 雖然最近基於 LLM 的代理可以識別源代碼中的許多候選錯誤，但它們的報告仍然是靜態假設，需要手動驗證，這限制了自動錯誤檢測的實用性。我們將這一挑戰框架設置為測試生成任務：給定一個候選報告，合成一個可執行的概念驗證測試，或簡稱 PoC——例如腳本、命令序列或精心製作的輸入——以觸發懷疑的缺陷。自動化的 PoC 生成可以作為可擴展的驗證神諭，通過提供具體的執行證據來實現端到端的自主錯誤檢測。然而，天真的 LLM 代理是不可靠的驗證者：它們偏向於「成功」，並可能通過生成看似合理但不具功能的 PoC 或甚至幻覺痕跡來進行獎勵駭客。為了解決這個問題，我們提出了 AnyPoC，一個通用的多代理框架，(1) 分析和事實檢查候選錯誤報告，(2) 迭代合成和執行 PoC，同時收集執行痕跡，以及 (3) 獨立重新執行和審查 PoC，以減少幻覺和獎勵駭客。此外，AnyPoC 還不斷提取和發展 PoC 知識庫，以處理異質任務。AnyPoC 在候選錯誤報告上運行，無論其來源如何，並且可以與不同的錯誤報告者配對。為了展示實用性和通用性，我們將 AnyPoC 與一個簡單的代理錯誤報告者應用於 12 個關鍵軟體系統，涵蓋多種語言/領域（許多系統擁有數百萬行代碼），包括 Firefox、Chromium、LLVM、OpenSSL、SQLite、FFmpeg 和 Redis。與最先進的編碼代理相比，例如 Claude Code 和 Codex，AnyPoC 為真陽性錯誤報告生成了 1.3 倍的有效 PoC，並拒絕了 9.8 倍的假陽性錯誤報告。迄今為止，AnyPoC 已經發現了 122 個新錯誤（105 個已確認，86 個已修復），其中 45 個生成的 PoC 被採納為官方回歸測試。
2604.11950.author: Zijie Zhao et.al.
2604.11950.authors: Zijie Zhao, Chenyuan Yang, Weidong Wang, Yihan Yang, Ziqi Zhang, Lingming Zhang
